reasoning: The school's authority clearly does not extend to 24/7 surveillance of students' personal devices outside of school hours. The fact that the principal is acting in direct contradiction to the district's published policy makes the lack of legitimacy obvious and indisputable to a reasonable observer.

The injustice is obvious because the Resident Advisor is explicitly exceeding their delegated mandate (physical safety) by demanding access to private digital property. No reasonable person would defend a dorm staff member's right to conduct warrantless digital searches of personal laptops, especially when the central authority (Housing Office) has confirmed this is prohibited.
